meaning: adj.衣衫褴褛的,寒酸的,懒散的n.懒散的女人,邋里邋遢的女人
examples: Her clothes were clean but dowdy.
It was about the small things too, like our dowdy polyester uniforms.
Mark Dowdy is another new addition to the Performance team, but he joined Iris in 1997 from Lotus.
He vows to remake the dowdy, 142-year-old church group into a savvy, fresh-faced marketer.
This evening, Bostich is driving a dowdy, gray minivan, a symbol of his other Tijuana life.
